steps.title: Define high-sinuosity rivers
steps.content: The requested definition should explain what makes a river high-sinuosity. The notes say a river with high sinuosity has many meanders, and another note defines a meander as a U-shaped curve in a river channel.

steps.title: Reject examples and extra details
steps.content: Choice A focuses on the Nestucca River, a specific example. Choice C explains how meanders form, not what high sinuosity means. Choice D adds later movement of meanders, which goes beyond a definition. The remaining choice gives the direct definition using many meanders. The answer is B.
